text: More temperature records have been broken as much of Europe continues to swelter in a heat wave.
zh: [0] 随着欧洲大部分地区持续遭受热浪侵袭，更多气温纪录被打破。

text: Here in the UK, the hottest temperature of the year was recorded on Thursday afternoon, with 36 .7 degrees recorded at Merrifield in Somerset.
zh: [1] 在英国，本年度最高气温于周四下午录得，索美塞特郡梅里菲尔德测得36.7度。

text: A rare red warning for extreme heat, meaning there's a danger to life, is currently in place in southern, central and western areas of England and parts of Wales.
zh: [2] 目前英格兰南部、中部、西部以及威尔士部分地区已发布罕见的极端高温红色预警，意味着生命安全受到威胁。

text: Some schools have closed, a hosepipe ban has been introduced in Kent and transport services have been disrupted.
zh: [3] 一些学校已停课，肯特郡实施了浇灌禁令，交通服务也受到影响。

text: In France, authorities say hospitals in Paris are overwhelmed and the government says the health alert has been raised to its highest level.
zh: [4] 法国官方表示，巴黎的医院不堪重负，政府称健康警报已升至最高级别。

text: New data shows Europe is the fastest warming continent in the world.
zh: [5] 新数据显示，欧洲是全球升温最快的大陆。

text: In fact, Europe is heating up twice as fast as the global average according to the Copernicus Climate Service.
zh: [6] 事实上，根据哥白尼气候服务机构的数据，欧洲的升温速度是全球平均水平的两倍。

text: Well, I'm joined now by Dr Chloe Brimacombe, a climate scientist who researches the impact of extreme heat and what policies countries can introduce in response.
zh: [7] 现在为我们连线的是克洛伊·布里马科姆博士，她是一位气候科学家，研究极端高温的影响以及各国可以采取的应对政策。

text: Thanks so much for being with us. Can you explain for us why Europe is increasing in its heat more than other parts of the world?
zh: [8] 非常感谢您做客我们的节目。您能解释一下为什么欧洲的升温幅度比世界其他地区更大吗？

text: So it's to do with the position of Europe.
zh: [9] 这与欧洲的地理位置有关。

text: So in the Northern Hemisphere,
zh: [10] 也就是在北半球，

text: so how the climate works is that we have three different cells around the world.
zh: [11] 气候运作的方式是全球存在三个不同的环流圈。

text: And basically the positioning of Europe means that we are more likely to see this faster growing heat on this continent.
zh: [12] 基本上，欧洲的地理位置意味着我们更可能在这个大陆上看到这种加速增长的高温。

text: But we also do see heat waves across the world.
zh: [13] 但我们在世界各地也确实看到了热浪。

text: So there was also a study that showed that nowhere in the world is safe from heat waves.
zh: [14] 有一项研究表明，世界上没有任何地方能免受热浪影响。

text: It's just a matter of time and a question of when a massive heat wave or records will be broken in all parts of the world.
zh: [15] 这只是时间问题，迟早世界各地都会出现巨大的热浪或打破纪录的高温。

text: But it's very pertinent in Europe because obviously of the positioning, it's the fastest warming.
zh: [16] 但这在欧洲尤为突出，因为显然由于其地理位置，它是升温最快的地区。

text: Heat waves Those in Europe are also caused by blocking high pressure systems and the jet stream, which is a
zh: [17] 欧洲的热浪也是由阻塞高压系统和急流引起的，急流是

text: fast moving body of air high up in the atmosphere.
zh: [18] 高空中快速流动的气流。

text: And this heat wave was partially caused by a divergent SINAR jet stream slowing down and making sure that this high pressure system could move in over Europe from North Africa.
zh: [19] 而这次热浪部分是由于一股分歧的急流减速，从而使这个高压系统得以从北非移入欧洲。

text: So, yes. And obviously, there's been a lot of impacts that we've seen over the last week.
zh: [20] 是的，显然，过去一周我们已经看到了很多影响。

text: and these weather warnings are
zh: [21] 而这些天气预警

text: continuing over the weekend,
zh: [22] 将持续到周末，

text: which is obviously concerning for continued impact.
zh: [23] 这显然令人担忧，因为影响仍在持续。

text: What would you warn people watching about how to look after themselves and the impact on the body?
zh: [24] 您想对正在收看的观众提出什么警示，关于如何照顾自己以及高温对身体的影响？

text: I was caught out myself with one of my children ending up in a St John's ambulance side room because he'd not drunk enough water and had overdone it in the heat because he just underestimated the impact of it.
zh: [25] 我自己也曾措手不及，我的一个孩子因为没有喝够水，在高温中过度活动，最终被送进了圣约翰救护车的临时观察室，因为他低估了高温的影响。

text: I think you put that quite clearly.
zh: [26] 我觉得您说得很清楚。

text: I think we can all very much underestimate the impact of heat.
zh: [27] 我认为我们都很容易低估高温的影响。

text: And a lot of the time we have been speaking about very much neededly that heat waves are silent killers.
zh: [28] 我们一直强调，热浪是无声的杀手，这一点非常重要。

text: So they can cause excess mortality in European countries.
zh: [29] 因此热浪会导致欧洲国家的超额死亡率上升。

text: And this is particularly true in the over 65s.
zh: [30] 这在65岁以上人群中尤为明显。

text: But everybody can be impacted and their health can be impacted by extreme heat.
zh: [31] 但每个人的健康都可能受到极端高温的影响。

text: and that's what the red weather warnings across Europe really talk to.
zh: [32] 而这正是欧洲各地发布红色天气预警的真正原因。

text: So it's also children, but you yourself if you don't drink enough water, if you don't stay cool, could experience heat stress and in the
zh: [33] 这不仅是孩子的问题，即使是你自己，如果不喝足够的水、不保持凉爽，也可能出现热应激，

text: most extreme case this can go on to cause a number of heat related illnesses, one of which is heat stroke.
zh: [34] 在最极端的情况下，这可能引发一系列与高温相关的疾病，其中之一就是中暑。

text: And actually if you do not access cooling as a result of heat stroke, what you might not realise is that you can actually have lasting brain damage as a result of experiencing too high temperatures.
zh: [35] 而实际上，如果中暑后没有及时降温，你可能没有意识到，这可能导致永久性的脑损伤。

text: So it's really important to stay cool
zh: [36] 所以保持凉爽真的非常重要，

text: and make sure that everybody around you is staying cool and safe as well.
zh: [37] 也要确保你周围的每个人都保持凉爽和安全。

text: And how much worse is it going to get and at what pace do you think?
zh: [38] 那么情况还会变得多糟，您认为速度会有多快？

text: I mean, how many more of these heat spikes will we see and how quickly?
zh: [39] 我是说，我们还会看到多少次这样的高温峰值，又会以怎样的速度出现？

text: The data from the UK Met Office shows that for the UK that within the next 12 years, and this was produced in 2022, which is when we last saw the 40 degrees Celsius record broken in the UK,
zh: [40] 英国气象局的数据显示，就英国而言，在未来12年内——这项数据是在2022年发布的，也就是英国上一次打破40摄氏度纪录的那一年——

text: that we were likely to see that again within the next 12 years.
zh: [41] 我们很可能会在未来12年内再次经历这样的高温。

text: And more research that was issued this week showed that we could be experiencing 45 degrees Celsius heat waves by 2056 if we don't reduce carbon emissions.
zh: [42] 本周发布的更多研究显示，如果我们不减少碳排放，到2056年我们可能会经历45摄氏度的热浪。

text: On Wednesday in the House of Commons, the UK government discussed the seventh carbon budget for the UK.
zh: [43] 周三，英国政府在下议院讨论了英国的第七个碳预算。

text: And luckily that was approved, which means that that legally binds the government to reducing emissions
zh: [44] 幸运的是，该预算获得了批准，这意味着政府将在法律上被要求

text: under a certain timeframe.
zh: [45] 在特定时间框架内减少排放。

text: So it's really encouraging to see that commitment, but hopefully that means that there will be actions on reducing these emissions so that heat waves don't continue to increase and get to those temperatures by 2056.
zh: [46] 看到这样的承诺令人鼓舞，希望这意味着将会有实际行动来减少排放，从而防止热浪在2056年前继续加剧、升温。

text: Dr Chloe Brimacombe, thank you very much indeed for being with us.
zh: [47] 克洛伊·布里马科姆博士，非常感谢您做客我们的节目。
